turns.text: Thanks for calling Sears Home Services — what appliance can I help you with today?
Hello? Sorry, I'm a bit hard of hearing. My oven is giving me trouble.
That's no problem at all — I'll speak clearly and take it slowly. What's the brand and model of the oven, and what's it doing?
It's a Frigidaire, model FFEF3054TS. The oven won't hold temperature, it started last week.

Thank you — a Frigidaire FFEF3054TS oven that won't hold its temperature since last week. Here's what to try: reset it by switching it off at the breaker for two minutes, then restore power and set a bake temperature. If it still drifts off temperature, the oven temperature sensor is the likely cause and worth a technician.
Sorry, could you say that again? I didn't quite catch it.

Of course, no rush. For your Frigidaire FFEF3054TS oven: switch it off at the breaker for two minutes, then turn the power back on and set a bake temperature. If it still won't hold the temperature after that, it likely needs a new oven temperature sensor, which a technician can replace.

Happy to. Step one: turn the oven off at the breaker… leave it off for two full minutes. Step two: turn the power back on, and set a bake temperature to test it. If your FFEF3054TS still can't hold the temperature, the temperature sensor is worn and a technician should replace it. Take your time with it.
Okay, I've got it now. Thank you for your patience.
You're very welcome — no trouble at all. If the reset doesn't hold it, call us back and we'll arrange a technician for the sensor. Take care.

steps_given: Reset the oven by switching it off at the breaker for two minutes, then restore power and set a bake temperature.
If it still won't hold temperature, arrange a technician to replace the oven temperature sensor.
